title: platform-user — internal capability flags
description: These are NOT tenant-facing entitlements — they are Academorix-internal capability flags that a super_admin toggles per PlatformUser to grant elevated permissions. Distinct from RBAC roles (which grant broad ability sets); entitlements are individual per-staffer capability gates that require explicit approval (usually via a separate ticket + super_admin signoff). Each entitlement grant is audit-logged with the granting actor + reason.

reference.publishedBy: access/rbac (entitlements substrate reused; the entitlements table itself is owned by that module)
reference.scoped_to: platform_admin guard only — tenant plane is a separate entitlements namespace

entitlements.description: Grants the holder the ability to start impersonation sessions against tenant Users (via the Wave 1b `access/delegation` module). Every impersonation start writes an audit row + an impersonation_sessions row + fires a `PlatformImpersonationStarted` event. This entitlement is granted per-PlatformUser (not per-role) so revocation is granular — a rogue actor cannot inherit impersonation from a role assignment.
typical_grantees: support agents on the platinum-support tier

entitlements.description: Grants the holder read access to the full cross-tenant audit trail (via /api/v1/platform/audits, owned by the audit module). Distinct from `platform.platform_users.view.audit_trail` — that latter is scoped to a specific PlatformUser's own actions. This entitlement is the broad compliance-review capability.
typical_grantees: external SOC 2 assessors (via a time-bounded PlatformUser row)

entitlements.description: Grants the holder the ability to perform admin actions on any tenant (change tenant status, disable a tenant, force-purge tenant data on request). Cross-tenant by construction. Every action writes both a platform-plane audit row AND a tenant-plane audit row (dual-writes so both the tenant DPO + the Academorix compliance officer see the same action).

Grants the holder access to security-sensitive tools (session revocation across the fleet, tenant PIN reset for locked-out admin scenarios, KMS key rotation triggers). This is the highest-privilege entitlement; a grant is a compliance-relevant event that pages the DPO within 24h.
entitlements.grantedBy: super_admin only, with a co-signer from the security department
typical_grantees: on-call incident responders during a Sev-1

grant_shape.description: Each entitlement grant writes a row to `platform_entitlement_grants` (owned by access/rbac, Wave 1b). The row records: platform_user_id, entitlement_key, granted_by_platform_user_id, reason, granted_at, expires_at (optional, for time-bounded grants), revoked_at (for revocation tracking). Grants are checked at request time in the controller layer via `EntitlementGate::allows($platformUser, 'platform.impersonate')`.
grant_shape.expiry: Grants may be time-bounded via expires_at. On expiry, EntitlementGate returns false + a scheduled command fires PlatformEntitlementExpired to notify the grantee + the granting super_admin.

notes: These are ORTHOGONAL to spatie-permission roles. A PlatformUser has role assignments (e.g. `ops` role = ops-baseline permissions) AND separately holds zero or more entitlements. The two layers compose — an `ops` PlatformUser without `platform.impersonate` cannot start an impersonation even if their role's default permission set included it.
Never grant these entitlements on a role — role-level grants are broadcast and lose the per-staffer audit trail. Always grant per-user.

For time-bounded grants (e.g. an external SOC 2 assessor gets `platform.audit_read` for the audit window), set expires_at explicitly + verify via the `platform-user:audit-report` command that no post-window access occurred.
